claim: The clean NGC1277-like relic baseline preserves the relic fit but is far below the high-Si/Mg regime.
notes: Use as the opening result: the successful relic model does not naturally explain Eftekhari-like Si/Mg.

claim: Generic reweighting with available code outputs cannot move the clean baseline toward the observed Si/Mg regime.
key_numbers: max |delta [Si/Mg]| from formed/surviving/remnant/code luminosity weights = 0.011 dex
notes: Current code luminosity is a simplified bolometric proxy, not an observed NIR band.

claim: E-MILES NIR-local weighting does not rescue the clean baseline, but it can amplify chemically Si-rich models.
key_numbers: clean baseline max [Si/Mg]=0.075; mild mixed case [Si/Mg]=0.311; largest chemically perturbed-model delta ~0.161 dex
paper_relevance: Important bridge to NIR observational comparison
notes: This is flux-weighted abundance averaging, not an abundance-response spectral fit.

claim: Public E-MILES baseFe SSPs reproduce baseline NIR index trends with age, metallicity, IMF slope, and velocity broadening, but do not isolate Si/Mg response.
notes: Useful as a baseline index inventory, not a full Eftekhari abundance inference.

claim: Low-Z initial Si pre-enrichment is diluted and leaves final stellar Si/Mg near the clean baseline.
paper_relevance: Rules out a simple initial-gas explanation
notes: Only Si was rescaled in a very-low-Z initial reservoir; more structured initial abundance patterns remain untested.

claim: A mild Si-rich component cannot reach nominal [Si/Mg]=0.67 by light mixing; the Si-rich component must already be near the target and dominate the relevant light.
key_numbers: mild [Si/Mg]=0.158 component requires 1194% of light for target 0.67; [Si/Mg]=0.67 component requires 100% for target 0.67
notes: Toy model mixes abundance ratios in linear space and converts back to dex.

claim: Simple mixed PopIII/PISN-like enrichment moves Si/Mg in the expected direction but either remains too low or degrades the relic fit when made aggressive.
key_numbers: mild m170 f0.1: code-light [Si/Mg]=0.158, E-MILES NIR~0.311; aggressive c100 z1 f0.3: code-light [Si/Mg]=0.508, E-MILES NIR~0.574, loss=20.097
notes: Copied-code experiment; not a production physical model.

claim: Timed early Si-rich enrichment can reach high Si/Mg while preserving relic constraints only up to a boundary near [Si/Mg]_NIR~0.5.
key_numbers: 20 analyzed local-refinement runs; accepted ceiling 0.499; 50 Myr counterpart rejected at 0.523
notes: Completed local batch does not contain any accepted model with [Si/Mg]_NIR >= 0.5.

claim: The best accepted model is timed_s0_d40_m170_f0p35_e1p00 and reaches the lower high-Si regime within the adopted relic-fit tolerance box.
key_numbers: E-MILES NIR [Si/Mg]=0.499; code-light [Si/Mg]=0.372; d[M/H]=+0.095; d[Mg/Fe]=-0.070; dlogM=+0.137; dlogM/L=+0.053
notes: Boundary solution; describe as final-enough for discussion, not publication-final without table verification.

claim: The 50 Myr timed counterpart reaches higher Si/Mg but fails the relic-fit tolerance box.
notes: Fails mainly through total metallicity, Mg/Fe, and stellar mass; M/L is less restrictive.

claim: Pushing Si/Mg upward usually perturbs multiple relic-fit observables at once rather than failing through one isolated constraint.
key_numbers: rejected [Si/Mg]>=0.4 rows: multiple failures dominate; highest rejected E-MILES NIR [Si/Mg]=0.574
paper_relevance: Explains why nominal 0.67 is not reached
notes: A successful channel needs to be Si-selective, localized, or short-lived without over-changing metal/mass budget.

claim: Relicness should correlate robustly with Mg/Fe and may correlate with Si/Mg only if an early Si-rich channel operated and avoided later dilution.
key_numbers: Prediction: Mg/Fe-relicness robust; Si/Mg-relicness conditional; normal ETGs can dilute toward [Si/Mg]~0
notes: Needs collaborator-facing wording and, eventually, observational comparison across relicness indicators.
